title: The Making of the Modern World
catalogDescription: Provides a global history of the present by investigating the relationships between political forces, economic processes, and socio-cultural dynamics of expansion, domination, and resistance. By looking into the past to understand the contemporary world, examines how the modern world came to be structured around nation-states, when most economies became capitalist, and why most societies are organized around structures of inequality. Offered: A.

title: Contemporary International Problems
catalogDescription: Explains contemporary international and global problems using interdisciplinary approaches. Focuses on the origins of current issues such as war, migration, poverty, trade, populism, climate change, technology, and other topics from the twentieth century up to today. Students learn to critically engage with the causes and potential solutions to problems facing the world today through research, writing, and discussion.

title: Culture and Power in International Studies
catalogDescription: Dives into how power, violence, and development shape the world, focusing on diverse cultures and histories. Explores how economic, political, and social backgrounds influence interactions and identities internationally. Students think critically about culture, examining how different groups use it today. Through case studies and social science methods, students gain new perspectives on how diversity plays a role in addressing global challenges.

title: Rise of Asia
catalogDescription: Key themes in the study of Asia, with focus on the present. Topics include: the notion of "Asia;" cultural and religious similarities and differences; comparison of colonial experiences under Western and Asian powers; World War II and liberation; postwar patterns of economic and political development; social patterns and issues. Offered: A.

title: Data and Social Change
catalogDescription: Covers how data shapes today's world and its role in tackling socio-economic challenges like education, health, and climate change. Reviews the basics of data analysis, combining statistical theory with real-world applications. Explores topics like misinformation, media bias, data manipulation, and privacy. Uncovers how to analyze data correctly and avoid misuse to create positive impacts on society and policy through case studies.

title: Claims and Evidence in International Studies Research
catalogDescription: Covers the methodology of international and global studies social science research. Introduces different methods of research including quantitative, qualitative, historical, and ethnographic. Offered: AWSp.

title: Data Ethnography: Artificial Intelligence, Decision-Making, and International Security
catalogDescription: Adapts ethnographic approaches to address problems of replicability, transparency, equity, bias, and ethics in work involving the generation, analysis, and use of big data and artificial intelligence in decision-making and international security. Students build skills to interrogate the social context of data through qualitative methods such as case studies, discourse analysis, document analysis, and fieldwork.
